data.title: Micro-Finance Program
data.full_content_html:
Micro finance Program is the core program of all DUS activities. DUS has been implementing its major program in partnership with Palli Karma Sahayak Foundation (PKSF) since 2000. It provides collateral free micro-credit to its around 40K+ group members where 97 percent are female.

Under this program, DUS has savings scheme for poor women who has no access in mainstream banks due to lack of capital and assets. Most of the targeted beneficiaries of DUS are poor women, marginal farmers and small micro entrepreneurs.

Major borrowers are women who used these loan funds to promote various income generating activities for their earnings and employments. As a result, micro finance program has positive impact on poverty reduction especially at grass root level, income enhancement, consumption, the promotion of rural businesses, education and health and finally the empowerment of women and their employment in rural island communities.

Key Achievements

  • 40,000+ active group members
  • 97% female beneficiaries
  • Over 95% loan recovery rate
  • Operating in 50+ villages
  • BDT 50+ crore distributed in loans

Impact on Community

The micro-finance program has significantly contributed to poverty reduction, women empowerment, and economic development in coastal communities. Many women have started their own businesses, improved their housing conditions, and are now able to send their children to school.

data.title: Climate Change and Disaster Management Program
data.full_content_html:
DUS is geographically located at very exposed disaster risk area of Coastal Bangladesh, most of its beneficiaries as well as core staffs of the organization and volunteers including general members are experienced by the influence of topography & living experience with the community, to cope with and face any natural disaster.

Further during its lifetime DUS was active in major emergency in relief and rehabilitation programs following Nov. 1970 cyclone relief, 1988/1991-cyclone recovery, 1998 flood response, SIDR 2007 etc.

DUS is now moving beyond relief and rehabilitation into institutionalized preparedness, risk reduction and management interventions as well as long term adaptation strategies as consequence of lessons learnt while helping communities cope with the devastating effects of Cyclone SIDR, which struck in November 2007.

Program Components

  • Early warning systems and community preparedness
  • Cyclone shelter management and maintenance
  • Climate-resilient agriculture practices
  • Post-disaster livelihood restoration
  • Coastal afforestation and mangrove protection

Our Response History

  • 1970 Cyclone Relief
  • 1988 Flood Response
  • 1991 Cyclone Recovery
  • 1998 Flood Response
  • 2007 Cyclone SIDR Response

data.title: Community Radio Program
data.full_content_html:
Strengthening Hatiya Island community for pioneering-connecting and empowering their Voice for Change. Bangladesh Government has already acknowledged the importance of community radio and announced the Community Radio Installation, Broadcast & Operation Policy.

Bangladesh is the 2nd country in South Asia in formulating policy for Community Radio. This initiative gives voice to the voiceless and empowers local communities to share their stories, concerns, and aspirations.

Broadcast Content

  • Agricultural information and weather updates
  • Health awareness and educational programs
  • Local news and community announcements
  • Women empowerment and youth engagement shows
  • Cultural preservation and local talent promotion
  • Disaster preparedness and emergency broadcasts

Community Impact

The community radio has become a vital source of information and entertainment for thousands of island residents. It has empowered local voices, preserved cultural heritage, and provided critical information during emergencies.

data.title: DWIP Education Program
data.full_content_html:
Education is the most powerful tool to break the cycle of poverty. DUS's Education Program focuses on providing quality education to underprivileged children in coastal communities.

The program includes formal education support, non-formal primary education for out-of-school children, scholarships for meritorious students, and adult literacy classes for women.

Key Interventions

  • Scholarship programs for 500+ students annually
  • School infrastructure development in remote areas
  • Teacher training and capacity building
  • Distribution of educational materials and supplies
  • Digital learning centers for rural students

Impact

Over 10,000 children have benefited from our education programs, with an 85% retention rate and significant improvement in learning outcomes. Many scholarship recipients have gone on to higher education and successful careers.

data.title: Information and Communication Technology (ICT)
data.full_content_html:
Bridging the digital divide in coastal communities through ICT for Development (ICT4D) initiatives. This program aims to empower communities with digital literacy and access to technology.

We establish community ICT centers, provide computer training, and facilitate access to online resources, government services, and digital financial inclusion.

Program Highlights

  • 15 community ICT centers established
  • Digital literacy training for 5,000+ women
  • Mobile banking and digital payment solutions
  • Online skill development courses
  • Telemedicine and e-health services

data.title: Research and Documentation
data.full_content_html:
DUS has a strong Research and Documentation Cell to conduct quality research in diverse areas of human and social development sectors, covering most importantly education, health, livelihood development, environment, human rights and social justice.

The R&D cell works as a professional support services unit to fulfill the growing demand for generation and systematic analysis of information in connection with the increasing involvement of DUS in its development activities.

Thus it engages in survey and research activities addressing the in-house needs of the organization for exploring and examining the feasible approaches for development, planning, designing, piloting, assessing and improving the implementation and performance of a wide range of projects.

Research Areas

  • Baseline surveys and needs assessments
  • Impact evaluation studies
  • Action research on poverty reduction
  • Documentation of best practices and case studies
  • Policy briefs and advocacy materials

data.title: Livelihood Restoration Project
data.full_content_html:
This project focuses on restoring and enhancing livelihood opportunities for communities affected by natural disasters and economic vulnerabilities.

We provide vocational training, asset transfer, and enterprise development support to help families rebuild their lives and achieve sustainable income sources.

Livelihood Options

  • Small business development and entrepreneurship
  • Agricultural diversification and improved farming
  • Livestock rearing and poultry farming
  • Fisheries and aquaculture development
  • Handicrafts and small-scale manufacturing

data.title: Group Member Insurance & Savings Scheme
data.full_content_html:
A comprehensive social protection mechanism for our group members, combining savings and insurance to provide financial security and risk coverage.

The scheme encourages regular savings habits while providing life insurance coverage, health benefits, and emergency support for member families during crises.

Benefits

  • Life insurance coverage up to BDT 50,000
  • Health and accident benefits
  • Emergency loan facilities from savings
  • Children's education support
  • Old age security and retirement benefits

data.title: Social Development Program
data.full_content_html:
Addressing social issues and promoting inclusive development through community mobilization and awareness programs.

The program focuses on social justice, gender equality, child protection, and community empowerment to create a more equitable society.

Focus Areas

  • Women empowerment and leadership development
  • Child rights and protection committees
  • Prevention of child marriage and dowry
  • Community-led social audits and accountability
  • Disability-inclusive development initiatives

data.title: Legal and Human Rights
data.full_content_html:
Promoting access to justice and human rights protection for marginalized communities through legal aid, awareness, and advocacy.

We provide free legal counseling, support for victims of human rights violations, and conduct workshops on legal rights and entitlements.

Services

  • Free legal aid and counseling clinics
  • Human rights awareness campaigns
  • Support for victims of violence and discrimination
  • Land rights and property dispute resolution
  • Paralegal training for community volunteers

data.title: WATSAN Program
data.full_content_html:
Building on its long experience of providing water and sanitation services to communities, DUS started its Water and Sanitation program with the financial and technical support of the Netherland Government.

The program is implementing in Nangolia Char and Nalerchar under Hatiya Upazilla. Our goal is to provide sustainable and integrated WATSAN services in the rural areas.

We break the contamination cycle of unsanitary latrines, contaminated water and unsafe hygiene practices, as well as ensure sustainability and scaling-up of WATSAN services.

Targets

  • 4,605 households with sanitation access
  • 250 deep tube wells for safe water
  • 20,000 people with hygiene education
  • Community-led total sanitation approaches
  • School WASH facilities and hygiene promotion

data.title: Training and Other Facilities
data.full_content_html:
DUS has developed a comprehensive Training and Communication Unit fully equipped with all possible physical and human resources to deliver high-quality capacity building programs.

We offer need-based training programs, develop modules and curriculum, and utilize modern training methodologies including participatory approaches, audio-visual aids, and practical demonstrations.

Training Offerings

  • Skill development and vocational training
  • Leadership and management training
  • Financial literacy and business management
  • ToT (Training of Trainers) programs
  • MIS and data management training

data.title: Tourism and Hospitality
data.full_content_html:
Promoting sustainable tourism and hospitality as an emerging livelihood opportunity for coastal communities, leveraging the natural beauty and cultural heritage of Hatiya Island.

This initiative focuses on developing community-based tourism, eco-tourism, and hospitality services that create employment while preserving local culture and environment.

Initiatives

  • Homestay and community guesthouse development
  • Tour guide training and certification
  • Handicraft and souvenir production
  • Local cuisine and food service training
  • Eco-tourism and nature conservation awareness
